operations.notes:
Returns a list of all alerts. If an empty list is returned, it implies that there are no configured alerts for that context.
You must set start and count parameters to limit the number of returned alerts.
Start is zero based - to start from the second rule use start=1, to get rules 51-60 use start=50 and count=10.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Returns the results beginning with the specified result number

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Returns the specified number of results

responseMessages.message: Value for start parameter is missing or not valid
Value for count parameter is missing or not valid

operations.notes:
Create an alert for unified security policies.
Returns location with the alert id to get the alert.
Alerts can be defined for all devices (all_devices:true) or for specified device IDs.
An email and/or user name must be defined. For multiple entries of emails or user names, use a semicolon to separate values. For SecureTrack, users in the collection are populated with "user_ids". The alert will be sent to the users (according to the addresses configured in SecureTrack) and to the emails.
To get the domain_id (optional parameter), call /domains/ API and use the ID included within the returned domain DTO.
At least one severity should be marked as 'true'.
There are no default values

responseMessages.message: User is not authorized to configure alerts for domain 'domainId'
Device ID specified does not exist or belongs to a different domain
No recipient details entered for alert
Invalid email address. Format must be name@domain.com
User is not authorized to configure alerts for Super Admin user: 'userName'
Username: 'userName' not valid for the specified domain

operations.notes:
Returns a specified alert.
If the alert_id does not exist, an error message is returned.

Update an alert for unified security policies.
Alerts can be defined for all devices (all_devices:true) or for specified device IDs.
An email and/or user name must be defined. For multiple entries of emails or user names, use a semicolon to separate values. For SecureTrack, users in the collection are populated with "user_ids". The alert will be sent to the users (according to the addresses configured in SecureTrack) and to the emails.
To get the domain_id (optional parameter), call /domains/ API and use the ID included within the returned domain DTO.
At least one severity should be marked as 'true'.
There are no default values

responseMessages.message: User is not authorized to configure alerts for domain 'domainId'
Device ID specified does not exist or belongs to a different domain
No recipient details entered for alert
Invalid email address. Format must be name@domain.com
User is not authorized to configure alerts for Super Admin user: 'userName'
Username: 'userName' not valid for the specified domain

operations.notes:
Delete specified alerts by IDs
To delete multiple alert_ids, use a comma between each ID

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Delete alerts in the specified domain ID.

responseMessages.message: Security Policy Alert Ids: :|'alert ids'| don't exist

all_devices.description: Marked if the alert is configured for all devices

send_syslog.description: True if alert is sent via syslog

low_severity.description: True if alert is applicable for low severity

medium_severity.description: True if alert is applicable for medium severity

high_severity.description: True if alert is applicable for high severity

critical_severity.description: True if alert is applicable for critical severity

device_id.description: Device IDs to be alerted on
